is a Canadian [sketch comedy](/wiki/Sketch_comedy "Sketch comedy") [troupe](/wiki/Comedy_troupe "Comedy troupe") formed in 1984 in [Calgary](/wiki/Calgary "Calgary") and [Toronto](/wiki/Toronto "Toronto"), consisting of [comedians](/wiki/Comedian "Comedian") [Dave Foley](/wiki/Dave_Foley "Dave Foley"), [Bruce McCulloch](/wiki/Bruce_McCulloch "Bruce McCulloch"), [Kevin McDonald](/wiki/Kevin_McDonald "Kevin McDonald"), [Mark McKinney](/wiki/Mark_McKinney "Mark McKinney"), and [Scott Thompson](/wiki/Scott_Thompson_(actor) "Scott Thompson (actor)"). Their [eponymous television show](/wiki/The_Kids_in_the_Hall_(TV_series) "The Kids in the Hall (TV series)") ran from 1989 to 1995, on [CBC](/wiki/CBC_Television "CBC Television"), in Canada. It also appeared on [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS"), [HBO](/wiki/Home_Box_Office "Home Box Office"), and [Comedy Central](/wiki/Comedy_Central "Comedy Central") in the United States.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Canadian comedy group

For the group's TV series, see [*The Kids in the Hall* (TV series)](/wiki/The_Kids_in_the_Hall_(TV_series) "The Kids in the Hall (TV series)"). For the hip hop duo, see [Kidz in the Hall](/wiki/Kidz_in_the_Hall "Kidz in the Hall").

| The Kids in the Hall | |
| --- | --- |
| At the 2008 eTalk Festival Party, during the [Toronto International Film Festival](/wiki/Toronto_International_Film_Festival "Toronto International Film Festival") (from left) [Dave Foley](/wiki/Dave_Foley "Dave Foley"), [Mark McKinney](/wiki/Mark_McKinney "Mark McKinney"), [Kevin McDonald](/wiki/Kevin_McDonald "Kevin McDonald"), [Bruce McCulloch](/wiki/Bruce_McCulloch "Bruce McCulloch"), [Scott Thompson](/wiki/Scott_Thompson_(actor) "Scott Thompson (actor)") | |
| Notable work | * *[The Kids in the Hall](/wiki/The_Kids_in_the_Hall_(TV_series) "The Kids in the Hall (TV series)")* * *[Kids in the Hall: Brain Candy](/wiki/Kids_in_the_Hall:_Brain_Candy "Kids in the Hall: Brain Candy")* * *[The Kids in the Hall: Death Comes to Town](/wiki/The_Kids_in_the_Hall:_Death_Comes_to_Town "The Kids in the Hall: Death Comes to Town")* |

| [Genres](/wiki/Comedic_genres "Comedic genres") | * [Surreal humor](/wiki/Surreal_humor "Surreal humor") * [sketch comedy](/wiki/Sketch_comedy "Sketch comedy") |
| Members | * [Dave Foley](/wiki/Dave_Foley "Dave Foley") * [Bruce McCulloch](/wiki/Bruce_McCulloch "Bruce McCulloch") * [Kevin McDonald](/wiki/Kevin_McDonald "Kevin McDonald") * [Mark McKinney](/wiki/Mark_McKinney "Mark McKinney") * [Scott Thompson](/wiki/Scott_Thompson_(actor) "Scott Thompson (actor)") |
|  | |
| Website | [kidsinthehall.ca](http://kidsinthehall.ca) |

**The Kids in the Hall** is a Canadian [sketch comedy](/wiki/Sketch_comedy "Sketch comedy") [troupe](/wiki/Comedy_troupe "Comedy troupe") formed in 1984 in [Calgary](/wiki/Calgary "Calgary") and [Toronto](/wiki/Toronto "Toronto"), consisting of [comedians](/wiki/Comedian "Comedian") [Dave Foley](/wiki/Dave_Foley "Dave Foley"), [Bruce McCulloch](/wiki/Bruce_McCulloch "Bruce McCulloch"), [Kevin McDonald](/wiki/Kevin_McDonald "Kevin McDonald"), [Mark McKinney](/wiki/Mark_McKinney "Mark McKinney"), and [Scott Thompson](/wiki/Scott_Thompson_(actor) "Scott Thompson (actor)"). Their [eponymous television show](/wiki/The_Kids_in_the_Hall_(TV_series) "The Kids in the Hall (TV series)") ran from 1989 to 1995, on [CBC](/wiki/CBC_Television "CBC Television"), in Canada. It also appeared on [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS"), [HBO](/wiki/Home_Box_Office "Home Box Office"), and [Comedy Central](/wiki/Comedy_Central "Comedy Central") in the United States.

The Kids made one film, *[Brain Candy](/wiki/Kids_in_the_Hall:_Brain_Candy "Kids in the Hall: Brain Candy")*, which was released in 1996. They reformed for various tours and comedy festivals in 2000. They later reunited for an eight-part miniseries, *[Death Comes to Town](/wiki/The_Kids_in_the_Hall:_Death_Comes_to_Town "The Kids in the Hall: Death Comes to Town")*, in January 2010. An eight-episode [revival season](/wiki/The_Kids_in_the_Hall_(season_6) "The Kids in the Hall (season 6)") was released on May 13, 2022, on [Amazon Prime Video](/wiki/Amazon_Prime_Video "Amazon Prime Video").[[1]](#cite_note-revival-1)[[2]](#cite_note-revival_broadcast-2) Their name came from 1950s TV comedian [Sid Caesar](/wiki/Sid_Caesar "Sid Caesar"), who would attribute a joke that did not go over well (or played worse than expected) to "the kids in the hall", referring to a group of young writers hanging around the studio.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-Debuts-5)

## Early history

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Kids_in_the_Hall&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early history")]

[Bruce McCulloch](/wiki/Bruce_McCulloch "Bruce McCulloch") and [Mark McKinney](/wiki/Mark_McKinney "Mark McKinney") were working together doing [Theatresports](/wiki/Theatresports "Theatresports") in [Calgary](/wiki/Calgary "Calgary"), performing in a group named "The Audience". [Norm Hiscock](/wiki/Norm_Hiscock "Norm Hiscock"), Garry Campbell and Frank Van Keeken were co-members, and they later became writers on the show. At the same time, [Dave Foley](/wiki/Dave_Foley "Dave Foley") and [Kevin McDonald](/wiki/Kevin_McDonald "Kevin McDonald") were performing around [Toronto](/wiki/Toronto "Toronto") (along with Luciano Casimiri) as The Kids in the Hall (KitH). In 1984, the two pairs met in Toronto and began performing regularly as KitH, with a rotating band of members, including [Paul Bellini](/wiki/Paul_Bellini "Paul Bellini") for a short time. When McKinney met [Scott Thompson](/wiki/Scott_Thompson_(actor) "Scott Thompson (actor)"), he invited him to join in January 1985 and the group had found its final form. The same year, McCulloch and Foley appeared in *[Anne of Green Gables: The Sequel](/wiki/Anne_of_Green_Gables:_The_Sequel "Anne of Green Gables: The Sequel")*, as [Diana Barry](/wiki/Anne_of_Green_Gables#Characters "Anne of Green Gables")'s husband (McCulloch) and a former classmate of Anne's from the fictional Queen's College (Foley).

Not long afterwards, the Kids broke up for a short time when scouts for *[Saturday Night Live](/wiki/Saturday_Night_Live "Saturday Night Live")* invited McKinney and McCulloch to New York to become writers for that show, and Foley made a poorly received film debut with *High Stakes*,[[6]](#cite_note-6) while Thompson and McDonald worked with the [Second City](/wiki/The_Second_City "The Second City") touring group. They were reunited in 1986. After *SNL*'s [Lorne Michaels](/wiki/Lorne_Michaels "Lorne Michaels") saw them perform as a troupe, plans began for a TV show. In 1987, Michaels sent them to New York to what was essentially a "Comedy Boot Camp". In 1988, their pilot special aired on [CBC Television](/wiki/CBC_Television "CBC Television"), and in the United States on [HBO](/wiki/HBO "HBO"), before debuting as a series in 1989.

## Television show

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Kids_in_the_Hall&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Television show")]

Main article: [The Kids in the Hall (TV series)](/wiki/The_Kids_in_the_Hall_(TV_series) "The Kids in the Hall (TV series)")

See also: [List of The Kids in the Hall episodes](/wiki/List_of_The_Kids_in_the_Hall_episodes "List of The Kids in the Hall episodes")

The series *[The Kids in the Hall](/wiki/The_Kids_in_the_Hall_(TV_series) "The Kids in the Hall (TV series)")* debuted as a one-hour [pilot](/wiki/Pilot_episode "Pilot episode") special which aired on [HBO](/wiki/HBO "HBO") and CBC Television in 1988[[7]](#cite_note-7) and began airing as a regular weekly series on both services in 1989.[[8]](#cite_note-8) The regular series premiered July 21, 1989, on HBO, and September 14 on CBC. In the United States, the first three seasons were on HBO before it moved to [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS") in 1993, where it stayed for two more seasons airing late Friday nights. CBC aired the show for the whole duration of its run.

In March 2020, it was announced that the series would return with an eight-episode season, on [Amazon Prime Video](/wiki/Amazon_Prime_Video "Amazon Prime Video"). The series features all five members, bringing back some of the show's classic characters and skits, and is executive-produced by Lorne Michaels. The revived series is billed as "the first Canadian Amazon Original series".[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[5]](#cite_note-Debuts-5) The revival was released on May 13, 2022.[[2]](#cite_note-revival_broadcast-2)

## *Brain Candy*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Kids_in_the_Hall&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Brain Candy")]

Main article: [Kids in the Hall: Brain Candy](/wiki/Kids_in_the_Hall:_Brain_Candy "Kids in the Hall: Brain Candy")

After the show ended its run, the troupe came together to produce a film, *[Brain Candy](/wiki/Kids_in_the_Hall:_Brain_Candy "Kids in the Hall: Brain Candy")*, featuring a few characters from the show and many new ones. Although not a commercial success, the film developed a cult following with their devoted fans.

## Tours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Kids_in_the_Hall&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Tours")]

### 2000 North American Tour

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Kids_in_the_Hall&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2000 North American Tour")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This section **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/The_Kids_in_the_Hall "Special:EditPage/The Kids in the Hall") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners") in this section. Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *(October 2017)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

In 2000, the troupe reformed for a successful North American tour, reprising many sketches from the show. The sketch line-up for the 2000 show was:

1. AT & Love Reunion
2. Mr. Heavyfoot Finds His Seat
3. Buddy Cole – The Year 2000
4. Cops!
5. Daddy's Dyin'
6. Head Crusher / Face Pincher
7. Jesus 2000!
8. Sir Simon Milligan & Hecubus in: The Pit of Ultimate Darkness
9. Gavin: Painting a chair
10. Comfortable
11. Sandwich People
12. Chicken Lady's Date
13. Power of the Suburbs
14. Bloody Salty Ham
15. Monologue by Brian on having a party when Fran and Gordon go on Vacation
16. Love Me
17. Fran: Brian's Bombshell
18. Jesus Christ Superstar
19. Encore: To Reg

At some shows:

1. Running Faggot
2. The Poker Game

The tour was chronicled in a documentary, *Kids in the Hall: Same Guys, New Dresses*, which followed the next year. This was then followed by the "Tour of Duty", and a DVD based on those performances, released in 2002.

### "Just for Laughs" ("Juste Pour Rire") Comedy Festival

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Kids_in_the_Hall&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: \"Just for Laughs\" (\"Juste Pour Rire\") Comedy Festival")]

In July 2007, the Kids reunited to perform at the 25th Annual "[Just for Laughs](/wiki/Just_for_Laughs "Just for Laughs")" ("Juste Pour Rire") Comedy Festival in [Montreal](/wiki/Montreal "Montreal").

The Just For Laughs show premiered about 90 minutes of new material. While certain characters made reappearances (Buddy Cole, Mr. Tyzik and McKinney and McCulloch's "smooth-talking" salesmen), the rest of the show revolved around entirely new material. Typically good-humored, the group poked numerous jokes at their own recent weight gain, and the state of their post-Kids acting careers.

Among the sketches:

* The Kids plan a new show. For the opening, they rape McDonald to the theme from *[Footloose](/wiki/Footloose "Footloose")*.
* Salesmen (McCulloch and McKinney) promote a device which can siphon fat from the American gut and use it to power [SUVs](/wiki/SUV "SUV").
* "Carfuckers": a group of mechanics who share a "love for which there is no name". The sketch was produced by an internet studio called "60Frames Entertainment".[[10]](#cite_note-10)
* Gavin encounters [Jehovah's Witnesses](/wiki/Jehovah%27s_Witnesses "Jehovah's Witnesses") (one of two sketches recreated from the television show).
* Foley and McDonald get drunk; Foley tells McDonald he has created a time machine with which he can "defeat last call".
* Foley travels back in time to receive [oral sex](/wiki/Oral_sex "Oral sex") from his wife (McCulloch), who would only perform the act on his birthday.
* Foley travels back in time to kill [Adolf Hitler](/wiki/Adolf_Hitler "Adolf Hitler") (Thompson) but instead accidentally inspires his [antisemitism](/wiki/Antisemitism "Antisemitism").
* Two exceptionally literate [rat-catchers](/wiki/Rat-catcher "Rat-catcher") (McCulloch and McKinney) look for a used futon.
* Buddy Cole speculates that [Jesus](/wiki/Jesus "Jesus") was homosexual.
* Kathy (McCulloch) and Cathy (Thompson) reunite for lunch in a restaurant, where Kathy extols the virtues of "tweeking" with [methamphetamine](/wiki/Methamphetamine "Methamphetamine").
* Foley is approached by a "fan" (Thompson) while waiting for the subway.
* A McCulloch monologue about how skinny [Nicole Richie](/wiki/Nicole_Richie "Nicole Richie") is.
* Foley and McCulloch fight over an imaginary girlfriend.
* The Chicken Lady has [phone sex](/wiki/Phone_sex "Phone sex") (one of two sketches recreated from the television show).
* The relationship woes of a gay couple (Foley and Thompson) are placated with the help of another married gay couple known as Peter and the Professor (McDonald and McKinney).
* Superdrunk: a [superhero](/wiki/Superhero "Superhero") who stops crimes by drinking (McCulloch), assisted by his trusty sidekick, the bartender (Foley).
* The show finished with Mr. Tyzik (McKinney) mocking the mannerisms and careers of each member of the troupe, after which he promptly crushed their heads.

The group also performed on January 26–27, 2008, at the SF Sketchfest. On January 26, there was a retrospective and Q&A with the group.

### 2008 North American Tour

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Kids_in_the_Hall&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: 2008 North American Tour")]

On April 4, 2008, The Kids in the Hall embarked on their first major national tour in six years. The tour ran through early June 2008, and included more than 30 cities in the US and Canada. The tour featured some material from the 2007 "Just for Laughs" performance, along with new material.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

The 2008 tour closely mirrored the "Just for Laughs" performance, excluding the rat catchers, subway fan and Nicole Ritchie sketches. In their stead, Mark McKinney performed the monologue titled "The Modern Hero" from Season 1 of the show, and the entire cast performed the sketch "This Is How I Danced in Tenth Grade."

## Other appearances

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Kids_in_the_Hall&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Other appearances")]

The cast members have reunited, in whole or in part, in numerous film and television works.

Kevin McDonald guest starred alongside Dave Foley, in [a 1997 episode](/wiki/NewsRadio_season_4#Stupid_Holiday_Charity_Talent_Show "NewsRadio season 4") of Foley's sitcom, *[NewsRadio](/wiki/NewsRadio "NewsRadio").*

Dave Foley and Kevin McDonald performed with the [Barenaked Ladies](/wiki/Barenaked_Ladies "Barenaked Ladies") on their "Ships and Dip V" cruise, along with other bands and comics, on February 1–6, 2009.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

All members of the Kids in the Hall voiced characters in the *[Lilo & Stitch: The Series](/wiki/Lilo_%26_Stitch:_The_Series "Lilo & Stitch: The Series")* episode "Fibber", with Kevin McDonald voicing [Pleakley](/wiki/Pleakley "Pleakley") and the other members voicing his family[[13]](#cite_note-13).

Dave Foley and Kevin McDonald later appeared as Jonathan Boy / All-American Boy and Professor Medula in the superhero film *[Sky High](/wiki/Sky_High_(2005_film) "Sky High (2005 film)")*.

In 2006, three of the members (McDonald, McCulloch and McKinney) appeared in the Christmas comedy *[Unaccompanied Minors](/wiki/Unaccompanied_Minors "Unaccompanied Minors")*, as The Guards in the Hall.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

The group appeared on the front cover of *[Naked Eye](/wiki/Naked_Eye_(magazine) "Naked Eye (magazine)")*'s summer 2008 edition.

The Kids performed at the 2008 Comedy Festival in [Las Vegas](/wiki/Las_Vegas_Valley "Las Vegas Valley") on November 22.[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16)

On August 6, 2010, all five Kids made an appearance on *[The Soup](/wiki/The_Soup "The Soup")* on [E!](/wiki/E! "E!"), to promote their miniseries *Death Comes to Town*. Four of the members appeared on-screen in drag (as girls), who had grown up as beauty pageant contestants (parodying *[Toddlers & Tiaras](/wiki/Toddlers_%26_Tiaras "Toddlers & Tiaras")*). McKinney's voice was heard off-screen as their mother.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

Foley was a voice actor on the 2011–2013 animated series *[Dan Vs.](/wiki/Dan_Vs. "Dan Vs.")*, which aired on [The Hub](/wiki/Discovery_Family "Discovery Family"). McDonald was a guest voice actor in one episode, "Dan Vs. Technology", in which he played technology guru Barry Ditmer (a parody of [Steve Jobs](/wiki/Steve_Jobs "Steve Jobs")).

In 2014, the cast reunited on Foley's sitcom *[Spun Out](/wiki/Spun_Out "Spun Out")*, appearing as Dave's high school goth friends who had made a suicide pact.[[18]](#cite_note-18) That year, Thompson also did a week-long stint as Buddy Cole (a producer Colbert doesn't know is gay) on *[The Colbert Report](/wiki/The_Colbert_Report "The Colbert Report")*, acting as the program's correspondent to the [2014 Winter Olympics](/wiki/2014_Winter_Olympics "2014 Winter Olympics") who covered [LGBT rights protests surrounding the 2014 Winter Olympics](/wiki/LGBT_rights_protests_surrounding_the_2014_Winter_Olympics "LGBT rights protests surrounding the 2014 Winter Olympics").[[19]](#cite_note-19) In the same year, the troupe performed a live reading of the screenplay for *Brain Candy* at the [Toronto Sketch Comedy Festival](/wiki/Toronto_Sketch_Comedy_Festival "Toronto Sketch Comedy Festival").[[20]](#cite_note-20)

In December 2014, McDonald, McKinney and Thompson appeared in an episode of the TVO's children's television series *[Odd Squad](/wiki/Odd_Squad_(TV_series) "Odd Squad (TV series)")*. The episode was entitled "Crime at Shapely Manor". McDonald appeared as Lord Rectangle, McKinney as General Pentagon, and Thompson as Professor Square.[[21]](#cite_note-21)

McDonald, Foley and Thompson appeared on [Chris Hardwick](/wiki/Chris_Hardwick "Chris Hardwick")'s game show *[@midnight](/wiki/@midnight "@midnight")*, which aired August 2, 2017 (season 4: episode 139).

*[The Kids in the Hall: Comedy Punks](/wiki/The_Kids_in_the_Hall:_Comedy_Punks "The Kids in the Hall: Comedy Punks")*, a documentary film by [Reginald Harkema](/wiki/Reginald_Harkema "Reginald Harkema"), premiered at the 2022 [Hot Docs Canadian International Documentary Festival](/wiki/Hot_Docs_Canadian_International_Documentary_Festival "Hot Docs Canadian International Documentary Festival").[[22]](#cite_note-22)

The group appeared on *[The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon](/wiki/The_Tonight_Show_Starring_Jimmy_Fallon "The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon")* on May 10, 2022.[[23]](#cite_note-23) They discussed the [revival of their show](/wiki/The_Kids_in_the_Hall_season_6 "The Kids in the Hall season 6") on Amazon Prime Video.

## *Death Comes to Town*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Kids_in_the_Hall&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Death Comes to Town")]

In July 2008, [Telefilm Canada](/wiki/Telefilm_Canada "Telefilm Canada") announced that there would be a new The Kids in the Hall television series, titled *[Death Comes to Town](/wiki/The_Kids_in_the_Hall:_Death_Comes_to_Town "The Kids in the Hall: Death Comes to Town")*.[[24]](#cite_note-24) Kevin McDonald stated that it would be an eight-part miniseries, airing first on CBC in Canada, and then on US television.[[25]](#cite_note-25) [Principal photography](/wiki/Principal_photography "Principal photography") took place from August 2009, in [Ontario](/wiki/Ontario "Ontario"). Several characters from the original *The Kids in the Hall* series made an appearance, including the OPP Officers and Chicken Lady. The first episode of the new series aired in Canada, on [CBC Television](/wiki/CBC_Television "CBC Television") on January 13, 2010. In the United States, the first episode aired on [IFC](/wiki/Independent_Film_Channel "Independent Film Channel") on August 20, 2010.[[26]](#cite_note-26)

## Awards and honors

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Kids_in_the_Hall&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Awards and honors")]

The group's 1988–1995 TV series won a number of awards, including the 1993 [Rose d'Or](/wiki/Rose_d%27Or "Rose d'Or") and the [Gemini Award](/wiki/Gemini_Award "Gemini Award") for Best Comedy Series in 1992 and 1993. It was nominated for various other awards, including the [Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Writing for a Variety Series](/wiki/Primetime_Emmy_Award_for_Outstanding_Writing_for_a_Variety_Series "Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Writing for a Variety Series") in 1993, 1994 and 1995.

In 2008, the group received a star on [Canada's Walk of Fame](/wiki/Canada%27s_Walk_of_Fame "Canada's Walk of Fame").

The group won the [Canadian Screen Awards](/wiki/Canadian_Screen_Awards "Canadian Screen Awards") Icon Award in [2019](/wiki/7th_Canadian_Screen_Awards "7th Canadian Screen Awards").[[5]](#cite_note-Debuts-5)
